title: Research Index
blurb: This directory contains research documents analyzing design decisions, exploring architectural trade-offs, and documenting reasoning for the Swift Institute ecosystem.

documents.topic: Architectural options for distributing PRIVATE_REPO_TOKEN (or its eventual replacement) across the swift-institute ecosystem at 1000+ org scale. Seeded 2026-05-06 after Option A (bot-driven read-only verification via organization_secrets:read) executed; remainder deferred to dedicated investigation. Four options stubbed: B (distribute App credentials to mint per-run tokens), C (bot writes secrets via organization_secrets:write), C-split (two-App separation of duties with separate secrets-bot), E (OIDC + external secret manager — Vault/Doppler/cloud-native). Common worst-case framing: App-key leak is catastrophic baseline regardless of option (existing contents:write across 17 orgs). Decision criteria: operational footprint at N=17/100/1000, threat-model blast radius, migration cost from Option A, required mitigations per option, terminal state + phased migration path. Sub-questions to verify empirically + prior-art targets enumerated. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide, IN_PROGRESS.
documents.statusDetail: v0.1.0 SEED. Authored after Option A (organization_secrets:read for bot-driven verification) executed 2026-05-06 via App-owner edit. Remainder deferred to dedicated session: full per-option operational and threat-model analyses, prior-art survey, terminal-state recommendation + phased migration. Resume per the §Outcome instructions.

documents.topic: Six-dimension read-only uniformity audit conducted post v1.1.1 rollout (2026-05-06) per principal directive. Verifies that the rollout's 'full uniformity' claim (scoped to ci.yml chain-routing, 0 stragglers) extends to the broader uniformity surface. D1 wrapper-file content: DELIBERATE-DIVERGENCE between L1 (5 layer-specific jobs per [CI-020]) and L2/L3 (thin pass-through per [CI-004a]); UNIFORM between L2 and L3 modulo layer-name strings + extensibility-hint comments. D2 caller routing: UNIFORM, 132+120+138=390 ci-routers and 390 docs-routers, zero drift. D3 metadata.yaml schema: UNIFORM, 390/390 with consistent description/topics/homepage three-key schema. D4 host-repo health files: UNINTENTIONAL-DRIFT — L2 + L3 hosts missing ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml + .swiftlint.yml + SECURITY.md vs L1 baseline; L3 also missing profile/README.md. Plus DELIBERATE-DIVERGENCE for swift-institute (umbrella role, runs Actions, hosts orchestrators/scripts/actions). D5 PRIVATE_REPO_TOKEN coverage: VERIFICATION-BLOCKED, my token lacks admin:org; defer to Sunday 2026-05-10 06:30 UTC lint-org-bot-coverage-weekly first cron fire. D6 sub-org wrapper presence: UNIFORM, 0/13 violations (correct per [CI-004b]). Counts: 3 UNIFORM, 1 DELIBERATE-DIVERGENCE primary + 1 partial, 1 UNINTENTIONAL-DRIFT, 1 VERIFICATION-BLOCKED. One remediation cohort proposed (Layer-host community-health sync, D4); one conditional cohort (App-permission-bump, D5 contingent on Sunday cron's axis-2 result). Stop conditions checked: D1 chain-routing-breaking — NOT triggered; D5 missing-secret — NOT triggered (cannot verify); >300 lines per dimension — NOT triggered. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

documents.topic: Comprehensive security review of the swift-institute + swift-primitives CI/CD stack across 5 scopes: (1) per-component matrix of every workflow / composite action / helper script / cron orchestrator with action SHA-pin state, secret scope, network egress surface, supply-chain dependencies, and token-leak surface; (2) threat model enumerating six attack paths labeled plausible-vs-speculative — T1 secrets exfil via fork-PR (closed by GitHub fork-secret rules + no `pull_request_target`), T2 write access via compromised reusable (closed by permissions intersection rule + branch protection IFF landed), T3 swift-institute-bot 17-org App-token escalation (chokepoint = branch protection on swift-institute/.github), T4 supply-chain via unpinned action / curl-piped helper / docker pull (T4a Dependabot for GitHub Actions partial mitigation noted), T5 shell-injection via repo-controlled content (mitigated by env-var indirection except in audit-step pattern which reduces to T2/T3 chokepoint), T6 speculative paths (cache poisoning, container escape); (3) gap inventory — 10 gaps spanning correctness / secret-leak / supply-chain / write-access-elevation classes, with G1 branch-protection identified as the single highest-priority remediation (collapses T2/T3/T5 to one chokepoint), G10 bot-installation-permissions audit added as NEW Cohort F; (4) branch-protection minimal proposal with exact `gh api -X PUT` shape per repo and three-wave provisioning order (host repos → 4 canaries → 128-repo fleet) including post-PUT diff-verification step + L2/L3 wrapper-host forward-reference, explicit per-action authorization gates per [CI-052]; (5) cohort sequencing producing 6-phase plan with 13 per-action authorization gates total over ~3 weeks. Most consequential finding: branch protection on swift-institute/.github + swift-primitives/.github is the single architectural chokepoint that closes the bot's 17-org write-elevation path; without it, anyone with push access to those two repos can mint contents:write tokens on any of 17 orgs via generate-metadata.yml. Top-level verdict: stack is in good security posture for its maturity (post Cohorts A1 + B Phase 1); remaining risk is dominantly chokepoint-resolvable. No new ecosystem primitives recommended ([RES-018] check); every cohort composes with existing infrastructure. Composes additively with ci-centralization-strategy.md (Path B / @main strategy), ci-cd-prior-art-and-pattern-survey.md (3 high-value candidates — 2 landed, 1 queued), and ci-cd-cross-ecosystem-reuse.md (lint-org-bot-coverage.yml extension noted; L2/L3 layer-wrapper repos join Wave 1 in next protection refresh). Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.
documents.statusDetail: v1.1.0. documents.topic: Cross-ecosystem CI/CD re-use across the 17-org Swift Institute manifest. Six-question investigation, v1.1.0 with principal correction: (Q1) L2/L3 wrapper — MUST exist mirroring layer orgs (codified as [CI-004a]); v1.0.0's no-wrapper verdict reversed because the org hierarchy IS the architectural primitive and CI hierarchy mirrors it; create swift-standards/.github + swift-foundations/.github with thin-pass-through wrapper files initially. (Q2) 11 L2 spec-authority sub-orgs — 3-tier through LAYER wrapper (sub-org repos route via swift-standards or swift-foundations); per-authority wrappers DEFERRED, structurally blocked by GitHub Actions workflow_call 4-level depth limit (current L1 chain per-package→layer-wrapper→universal→advisory-linter is at the limit; adding sub-org wrapper would exceed); codify as [CI-004b] (sub-org wrappers are future work, sunsets when GitHub raises limit OR universal is refactored to inline advisory linters). (Q3) Cross-org bot + secret distribution — existing mechanism correct (per-org App install + central client-id pair + per-org PRIVATE_REPO_TOKEN per [CI-060]); add lint-org-bot-coverage.yml advisory linter. (Q4) Third-party re-use — unintended-but-permitted, disclaimer in universal header. (Q5) Bot's expanded role — current services scale via orgs.yaml filter expansion; no L2/L3-specific service clears [RES-018]; one new META service (lint-org-bot-coverage.yml) with THREE coverage axes (App install + org secret + NEW v1.1.0 wrapper-file presence). (Q6) Migration — THREE-axis (v1.0.0 was two-axis): (A) create L2+L3 layer wrappers, (B) migrate ~280 consumer per-package ci.yml callers to route through layer wrappers, (C) phased orchestrator-filter expansion; 11 phases plus future option (Phase 12+) for per-authority wrappers post-limit-relief. Most consequential finding: principal correction reframes question from 'what invariants justify a wrapper?' to 'what does org hierarchy require?'; layer wrappers exist unconditionally at every layer org as anchor for future accretion. 4-level workflow_call limit is a binding architectural constraint preventing full org-hierarchy mirroring at sub-org level today. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

documents.topic: Comparative prior-art survey of CI/CD patterns across 6 non-Swift ecosystems (Rust, Go, Python, JS/TS, multi-language large-OSS, plus Apple's swift_package_test reference). Cross-ecosystem synthesis identifies universal patterns (composite actions for setup recipes, scheduled-cron sweeps, harden-runner egress lockdown, OSSF Scorecard, OIDC keyless publishing), common patterns (workflow_call reuse, conclusion-aggregator job, hot-path/cold-path split, sharded testing, generated workflows from typed source), and convergent absences (layered package-tier organization, maintainer-operated cross-repo cron orchestrator, public/private visibility gate — all swift-institute-original design). Application to Swift Institute identifies 3 high-value candidate cohorts: (1) step-security/harden-runner egress lockdown (closes the supply-chain gap left by SHA-pinning declination); (2) Rust-style conclusion-aggregator job (decouples branch protection from workflow refactors); (3) OIDC keyless attestation for tagged releases via sigstore/cosign (establishes signing infrastructure ahead of SwiftPM Trusted Publishers). 3 deferred candidates (hot-path/cold-path split, swift-tools-version derivation, DockerHub→ghcr.io mirror) — analogs don't fit cleanly or trigger condition not yet present. 3 declined candidates (generated workflows from typed source, external CI, external dashboards) — appropriate at scales swift-institute hasn't reached. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.
documents.statusDetail: Survey produces 3 high-value candidate cohorts pending principal authorization for next-cohort sequencing

documents.topic: Phase 1 deployment-readiness analysis for the three deterministic γ-class advisory reusable workflows specified in `centralized-swift-ci-and-spine-gate.md` v1.2.0 §3.4.2 / §3.4.3 / §3.4.5: γ-1a Foundation-family import enforcement, γ-1b License-header advisory (Stage 1 of three), γ-2 Mechanical hygiene (YAML lint + broken-symlink, separate reusables per §3.4.11). Originating dispatch (`HANDOFF-workflow-construction-phase-1.md` 2026-05-05) framed phase 1 as BUILD; on inspection, the four reusables (`lint-foundation-family-import.yml` + `lint-license-header.yml` + `lint-yaml.yml` + `lint-broken-symlink.yml`) had already landed (commits `ea4128f`, `6c3d726`, `2480628`), conformed to v1.3.0 §3.5 corrections (advisory-as-input pattern, ubuntu-latest no-container, shell:bash explicit), and were canaried green on swift-carrier-primitives + swift-tagged-primitives. Per supervisor block `ask:` clause, dispatch escalated and reframed AUDIT + TEST + DOCUMENT. Doc covers: (a) per-spec-clause conformance audit against v1.2.0 + v1.3.0 (every clause tabulated; all conformant with one-paragraph notes on each non-obvious match like the bare `#if canImport(Foundation*)` shape and the §3.4.5 'consolidated-issue vs separate-reusable' distinction); (b) empirical positive/negative scratch-repo verification — 8 fixtures (4 γ-classes × positive + negative) authored at /tmp/wf-phase1-scratch with a harness mirroring each workflow's gating shell; all 8/8 produce expected exit codes (γ-1a positive: 6 errors + 1 warning across 5 attribute permutations including @_exported/@preconcurrency/@_implementationOnly/package-import + Tests/Support ERROR + Tests/** WARNING; γ-1a negative: zero false-positives on `import Swift` / `import FoundationFooBar` / comment-imports / string-literals; γ-1b positive: 2 missing across no-header + MIT-header; γ-1b negative: zero false-positives on swiftlang-style + SPDX-Identifier headers + Tests/** + Package.swift + Package@*.swift exclusion; γ-2a yamllint catches indentation + line-length + dependabot indentation; γ-2b find -L detects absolute + relative dangling symlinks); (c) per-rule inventory mapping (γ-1a → [CI-022] + [PRIM-FOUND-001]; γ-1b → [GH-REPO-040]/[GH-REPO-041]; γ-2 → [CI-053]/[GH-REPO-060]/[CI-031]); (d) phase 1.5 deployment plan — clarification that phase 1.5 is NOT per-consumer caller fan-out (those exist already from B7c-B8d wave); the four reusables are auto-rolled-out via universal swift-ci.yml `[CI-031]` transitive ride; the actual phase 1.5 graduation is a one-line edit at universal swift-ci.yml's calling site (drop `with: { advisory: true }`) gated on 2 consecutive clean weekly cron sweeps + principal-side audit clean per §3.4.10; recommended wave order γ-2a → γ-2b → γ-1a → γ-1b stage 2 codemod → γ-1b stage 3 gate flip. Dispatch text mismatches: prescribed single `mechanical-hygiene.yml` (over-aggregated; existing two-file split is §3.4.11-conformant), prescribed SHA-pin all third-party actions (over-strict; existing `actions/checkout@v6` tag pin is [CI-013] + feedback_latest_versions_only.md conformant; SHA-pin reserved for niche actions per §3.4.5b precedent), prescribed `*-check.yml` filenames (existing `lint-*.yml` matches sibling convention). No workflow file modified; no remote pushed; existing implementation determined deployment-ready.

documents.topic: Sub-classification of the verification-taxonomy mechanical-bucket rules that apply to swift-primitives Swift code into four enforcement-tool buckets: swift-format / swiftlint-canonical / swiftlint-custom / custom-ci. Inputs read in full at write time: pilot + tier-1 + tier-2 + tier-3 classification docs (195 unique mechanical rules across 15 in-scope skills — code-surface 15 + primitives 4 + documentation 32 + existing-infrastructure 13 + swift-package 5 + swift-package-build 6 + conversions 18 + index 6 unique + memory-arithmetic 8 + memory-safety 27 + modularization 13 + platform 22 + swift-package-heritage 2 + testing 15 + testing-institute 9), swift-primitives root .swift-format (1907-byte JSON; 20 active swift-format rules + 14 disabled including ValidateDocumentationComments), canonical SwiftLint config at swift-standards/swift-standards/main/.swiftlint.yml (17 disabled + 9 opt-in + function_parameter_count tuning), Apple swift-format Rule Documentation (closed catalog of 43 rules: 18 linter + 25 formatter), SwiftLint Rule Directory (closed catalog of 324 rules: 129 default + 190 opt-in + 5 analyzer). Aggregate distribution: 1 swift-format (0.5%) + 1 swiftlint-canonical (0.5%) + 110 swiftlint-custom (56.4%) + 83 custom-ci (42.6%). Already-enforced count: 0 (the existing swift-primitives .swift-format active rules + canonical SwiftLint opt-in rules cover orthogonal formatting concerns; zero mechanical-bucket overlap with the in-scope set). Config-flips: 2 cheapest-path rules — [API-IMPL-005] One Type Per File via swiftlint-canonical one_declaration_per_file opt-in flip (single canonical-config edit; no fanout), [DOC-003] Method documentation via swift-format ValidateDocumentationComments flip from false to true (Fanout-Required: 133 sub-package .swift-format copies). Custom-rule-author cost characterization across 110 swiftlint-custom rules: ~25 one-liner regex (cheap, e.g., [API-ERR-001] typed-throws regex, [INFRA-020] Int(bitPattern:) trigger, [PRIM-FOUND-001] Foundation imports, [PLAT-ARCH-008c] L1 #if-os ban), ~55 moderate AST queries (e.g., [API-ERR-002] nested error types, [MEM-COPY-001] ~Copyable decl-site, [IDX-006] operator overload signatures), ~30 complex multi-decl / multi-rule clusters (e.g., [INFRA-200] operations-intentionally-missing closed pattern list, [CONV-003-006] API-table multi-pattern, [PKG-NAME-002/006] namespace+protocol+typealias triplets, [MEM-REF-002-005]/[MEM-LIFE-002-007] reference+lifetime primitive site enforcement). Custom-CI residual concentrated in: 27 Package.swift parsing (most of modularization + swift-package-build Linux family + [PRIM-ARCH-001/002] + nested-test-package rules), 30 DocC markdown / .docc file parsing (essentially all of documentation except [DOC-003] Swift doc-comments and [DOC-045] Swift // WORKAROUND: comments), 14 cross-package grep / cross-repo enumeration (most of platform PLAT-ARCH-* family), 5 build/shell invocations, 2 git-log/dependabot, 2 pre-commit hooks, 1 workflow-grep, 2 diff-aware predicates ([API-IMPL-010] visibility-widening trigger + [PKG-NAME-008] post-rename shadow scan). γ-roadmap migration candidates in scope: 1 — [PRIM-FOUND-001] No Foundation Imports = γ-1a deterministic in v1.2.0 §3.4.10, currently implemented as workflow YAML, could migrate to a one-liner SwiftLint custom rule in canonical .swiftlint.yml (parent_config: distribution removes per-package fanout cost); below the 5-rule cluster threshold, no coordinated migration dispatch warranted. γ-1b license-header is OUT OF SCOPE for this dispatch (readme skill excluded). No 5th-bucket (swiftsyntax-tool) candidates surfaced — every rule routed cleanly into the four buckets. Phasing recommendation (informational): Phase 1 config-flips (3 rules total counting γ-1a migration; minutes-to-hours), Phase 2 one-liner regex custom rules (~25 rules, low cost), Phase 3 moderate AST custom rules (~55 rules), Phase 4 complex multi-decl custom rules (~30 rules), Phase 5 custom-CI for Package.swift / cross-package / DocC / build-shape rules (~83 rules). Out of scope per supervisor block: any config file modification, PR drafts, workflow YAML drafts, γ-1a migration execution, fanout-mechanism work, generalization beyond swift-primitives, mechanical/hybrid/semantic re-classification.
documents.statusDetail: v1.0.0. Sub-classification dispatch closes the swift-primitives-scoped subset of the verification-taxonomy mechanical-bucket. 195 unique mechanical rules sub-classified into 4 enforcement-tool buckets with annotations (Already-Enforced / Config-Flip / Custom-Rule-Author / Fanout-Required / γ-Migration-Candidate / Tool-Alternative). Distribution heavily favors swiftlint-custom (56.4%) reflecting that most in-scope rules are AST/regex on Swift source; custom-ci (42.6%) concentrates in Package.swift parse, DocC markdown, cross-package grep, build/shell. Config-flip count 2 = strict-win immediate-coverage path. γ-roadmap migration candidate count 1 (PRIM-FOUND-001 = γ-1a Foundation, suspected migration to swiftlint-custom). No 5th bucket needed. No supervisor block ask: triggers fired (no irreconcilable conflicts; <5 γ candidates; ~200 in-scope total marginal-overage compliant per [SUPER-024]; fanout divergence out of dispatch scope). Implementation gated per supervisor block 'MUST NOT author or modify any config file' and 'MUST NOT draft any PR'; this RECOMMENDATION is the sole artifact. Generalization to other ecosystems (swift-foundations, swift-standards, etc.) is a future dispatch gated on this RECOMMENDATION's review.

documents.topic: Phase 1 rollout of the centralized linter harness for swift-primitives. Lands 3 mechanical rules: [DOC-003] ValidateDocumentationComments (swift-format catalog rule, fanout via new sync-swift-format.sh + swift-format-canonical.json) + [API-IMPL-005] one_declaration_per_file (SwiftLint built-in opt-in flip at Tier 1) + [PRIM-FOUND-001] Foundation-family imports (γ-1a workflow migration to Tier 2 SwiftLint custom rule pair). Architecture: 3-tier parent_config: chain — Tier 1 ecosystem-wide canonical at swift-institute/.github/.swiftlint.yml (NEW), Tier 2 swift-primitives org-specific canonical at swift-primitives/.github/.swiftlint.yml (NEW; parent_config Tier 1), Tier 3 per-sub-package overrides (132 files re-targeted from the no-longer-canonical swift-standards/swift-standards URL to the new Tier 2 URL). Apple swift-format has no remote inheritance so canonical .swift-format distributed via fanout (134 files updated by sync-swift-format.sh, mirroring sync-dependabot/sync-swift-settings family pattern). γ-1a migration: deleted lint-foundation-family-import.yml + lint-foundation-family-import-weekly.yml + audit-foundation-import.py + swift-ci.yml caller block + cron-audit-base.yml consumer-list comment reference. Peer CI/CD agent caught the weekly cron deletion (which I'd missed in the original draft); the weekly's cross-org drift-sweep purpose is absorbed by Tier 2 per-PR enforcement. Regex parity verified 24/24 cases (11 attribute permutations + 6 variants + 7 negatives) between deleted Python audit script and new Tier 2 SwiftLint custom rules. Two live user revisions during dispatch: (1) premise correction — swift-standards/swift-standards no longer canonical, re-derived to 3-tier chain at .github org-special repos; (2) source-refactor scope deferred — user explicitly OVERRODE supervisor block 'MUST fix every existing violation' with 'focus on CI; DO NOT exclude to fix; failing CI is expected; we welcome the violations as these point us to where we need to make changes.' Baseline violation counts (the intentional diagnostic surface): [DOC-003] 155 across 33 packages (swift-format linter; not auto-fixable), [API-IMPL-005] 23 across 6 packages (below supervisor codemod-threshold of 30; manual file splits in scope for Phase 2+), [PRIM-FOUND-001] 79 across 6 packages (top: swift-render-primitives 51 + swift-structured-queries-primitives 12 + swift-test-primitives 11; vs brief's expected zero — γ-1a was advisory not gating, so violations accumulated unenforced). Total = 257 violations expected to fail CI on first push wave; intentional. 5-wave push plan: W1 swift-institute/.github (commit 7f53280, 6 files), W2 swift-primitives/.github (commit 8a03db6, 1 file), W2.5 single-canary on swift-carrier or swift-tagged for live HTTP/cache/CDN verification, W3 132 sub-repo commits (uniform 'parent_config re-target + ValidateDocs flip'), W4 workspace-level root files (NOT a git repo; no push), W5 Research repo (this doc + index entry). Phase 2+ source-refactor cleanup is the follow-up dispatch chain.

documents.topic: Tier 2 swift-primitives-scoped enforcement-rule design grounded in empirical baseline of current Sources/. Maps user directive 'move away from bitPattern and unchecked inits, stay high typed, prefer map and retag' onto the SwiftLint Tier 1/Tier 2 canonical configs landed in Phase 1.5 / Tier-A. Empirical baseline (parallel-subagent grep, file-level counts, methodology per [RES-023]): Int(bitPattern: 180 files (mixed: bare form is the [INFRA-002] integration overload; chained .rawValue form is the anti-pattern); __unchecked: 41 files (mixed: declaration-site legitimate, call-site anti-pattern); .rawValue. chains 12 files; count - 1 34 files; Cardinal(0)/(1) 2 files (almost clean); Swift.min(...rawValue) 0 / count -= 1 0 / count &-= 1 0 / position.rawValue + 0 (already-clean discipline). Cohort design recommends 4 LAND rules (R1 cardinal_count_minus_one_anti_pattern regex `\bcount\s*-\s*1\b` warning ~50-100 hits cite [INFRA-025]; R2 cardinal_zero_one_constructor_anti_pattern regex `\bCardinal\(\s*[01]\s*\)` warning 2 hits cite [INFRA-101]; R3 chained_rawvalue_access_anti_pattern regex `\.rawValue\.\w+` warning 12 hits cite [INFRA-103]; R4 bitpattern_rawvalue_chain_anti_pattern regex `Int\(bitPattern:\s*[^)]*\.rawValue` warning ~30-80 hits cite [INFRA-002]/[CONV-016]) all at Tier 2 (primitives-specific, mirrors no_foundation_import_*/l1_no_platform_conditionals architectural placement; would be wrong at Tier 1 because Cardinal/Tagged-rawValue are primitives-specific types). 2 DEFER rules to SwiftSyntax-based tooling (R5 __unchecked: at call sites only — needs call-site-vs-decl-site AST distinction; R0 positive enforcement — needs conversion-tier classification per ai-context-reduction-via-type-system-tooling.md Option D Symbol-graph pipeline). 3 SKIP rules (R6 bare Int(bitPattern:) IS the canonical [INFRA-002] integration overload; R7 Swift.min(rawValue) and R8 position.rawValue + arithmetic both 0 hits already clean). Total expected diagnostic surface on landing: ~80-130 violations concentrated in swift-buffer-primitives, swift-tree-primitives, swift-ordinal-primitives, swift-hash-table-primitives. Failing CI is the intended diagnostic per user posture established Phase 1. Composability with existing Tier 2 (no_foundation_import_*, l1_no_platform_conditionals): all five primitives-specific Tier 2 rules share reach (132 sub-packages via parent_config:) and severity model (warning + --strict CI gating).

documents.topic: Tier 2 ecosystem-wide prior-art survey (parallel-subagent verified per [RES-020]) of Swift Wasm CI strategy after γ-3's commit `8e3a76c` rollout surfaced a 6.3.0-SDK ↔ 6.3.1-compiler ABI mismatch. Six sub-questions: (Q1) SDK distribution model — swift.org canonical since Swift 6.2 (2025-09-15); every 6.2.x and 6.3.x patch has a Wasm SDK with checksum exposed via `https://www.swift.org/api/v1/install/releases.json`; no published guarantee but strong empirical pattern; community swiftwasm/swift-sdk-index has gaps (no 6.3.1) and is not a viable canonical source; swiftly does not yet handle Wasm SDKs. (Q2) Embedded vs Wasi disambiguation — three concepts: the `-enable-experimental-feature Embedded` compiler flag, the `_wasm` SDK ID (full stdlib + WASI), and the `_wasm-embedded` SDK ID. CRITICAL FINDING: both SDK IDs target the SAME `wasm32-unknown-wasip1` triple; embedded is NOT bare-metal; the `embedded-toolset.json` automatically passes `-enable-experimental-feature Embedded -static-stdlib -wmo -D__EMBEDDED_SWIFT__` to swiftc when `_wasm-embedded` is selected. The `_wasm-embedded` variant is the closer fit for swift-primitives' L1 freestanding-buildability invariant. (Q3) Stable-vs-nightly framing in v1.1.0 §3.4.6 was based on incomplete swift-syntax read — the actual swift-syntax Wasm matrix is `["nightly-main", "nightly-6.3", "6.2"]` (or 6.3 in newer reusable versions), both stable AND nightly together, hard-gated. 6 of 7 surveyed Apple packages (foundation/collections/numerics/system/async-algorithms/argument-parser) use the swiftlang/github-workflows reusable; swift-nio is the lone holdout with bespoke `wasm_swift_sdk.yml`. ALL hard-gated; only swift-collections uses the embedded variant alongside wasi. (Q4) Job structure — three options analyzed: α status quo + dynamic resolution (RECOMMENDED), β adopt swiftlang reusable as sub-job, γ full migration. Recommendation: Option α — replace hardcoded SDK URL+checksum with curl/python3 lookup against `releases.json` (sketch provided). Keeps two-job shape (`embedded` Linux nightly + `embedded-wasm-sdk`); do NOT add wasi-variant job at L1 (does not test L1 invariant). (Q5) Apple precedent — Wasm CI is now table stakes; dominant pattern is the swiftlang reusable; Institute starting advisory is more conservative than any Apple package; destination state should match industry hard-gating. (Q6) Soak taxonomy — extend class C with C.1 (install network/availability) vs C.2 (SDK ↔ toolchain ABI mismatch); §3.5.7's incident reclassifies B → C.2; if Option α is adopted, C.2 becomes empty by construction. Open follow-ups: per-L1-package empirical embedded-on-Wasm-WASI verification, L2/L3 layer wrappers' future Wasm decisions, swiftly Wasm-SDK support, Embedded compiler-flag stabilization.
documents.statusDetail: v1.0.0. Recommendation: adopt dynamic SDK resolution from `releases.json` (single-commit edit to `swift-primitives/.github/.github/workflows/swift-ci.yml` `embedded-wasm-sdk` job's `Install Embedded Wasm SDK` step) — implementation gated per feedback_no_public_or_tag_without_explicit_yes. Keep `_wasm-embedded` SDK variant (matches L1 freestanding-buildability invariant; non-suffixed `_wasm` would not). Keep two-job shape; do not add wasi-variant at L1. Maintain γ-3 advisory through 4-week soak; flip to hard-gate to match Apple ecosystem default. Extend soak taxonomy with C.1/C.2 sub-class; recommend §3.5.7 incident be reclassified B → C.2 in centralized doc's next revision. No code change executed in this Phase 1 (research-only per parent handoff scope).

documents.topic: Tier 2 cross-package recommendation closing the L1 layer violation flagged in swift-async-primitives' 0.1.0 brief Phase 1 §#4. Investigation Q1 finding (verified 2026-05-04): the Kernel_Thread_Primitives fallback at Async.Mutex.swift:149-157 is unreachable. Four independent lines of evidence converge: (A) the precise module name Kernel_Thread_Primitives exists nowhere in the ecosystem — thread-mutex types DO exist at L2 (ISO_9945_Kernel_Thread, final class with manual lock/unlock) and L3 (Thread_Synchronization in swift-foundations/swift-threads, Kernel_Thread in swift-foundations/swift-kernel), but those are different module identifiers and incompatible API shapes (class with manual locking vs struct with withLock closure); (B) Synchronization is unconditionally available on every host that can build the package given Swift 6.3.1 toolchain floor + Swift 6 language mode + .v26 platforms — branch 2 covers all non-Apple, non-embedded targets; (C) zero callers, zero test coverage, undocumented in the package's DocC Semantics.md contract; (D) every in-package Async.Mutex consumer (Promise, Completion, Broadcast, Channel.Bounded, Channel.Unbounded, Publication, Semaphore via dependency, Waiter via Atomic) directly imports Synchronization separately for Atomic<T> state machines — the fallback is architecturally incoherent because a hypothetical Synchronization-less target would compile branch 3 and then fail every other consumer. Recommendation: Option 1 — single-commit deletion of lines 149-158 + cosmetic Kernel.Handoff.Cell doc-comment reference at Async.Publication.swift:63 + README §41 swift-kernel-primitives dep-list entry. Four rejected alternatives enumerated: Option 2 wire as real dep (creates a layer violation), Option 3 relocate to L3 (Q3+ path, not Q1's stop-condition), Option 4 leave alone (does not close brief §#4), Option 5 retarget at swift-threads/ISO_9945_Kernel_Thread (still a layer violation AND breaks the Mutex API surface — class-with-manual-locking vs struct-with-withLock). Stop-condition compliance per the handoff's Q1-vestigial branch: Q2/Q3/Q4 deliberately not investigated; the 4 L1 consumers (cache, pool, render, test) require no migration. Secondary observation tracked separately: surviving Darwin os_unfair_lock branch is platform-conditional code at L1 and would be a [PLAT-ARCH-008c] concern under strict reading; out of scope for Option 1 per handoff's secondary-concern framing. Execution remains gated per feedback_no_public_or_tag_without_explicit_yes.
documents.statusDetail: v1.1.0. Recommendation: Option 1 — single-commit deletion of the vestigial Kernel_Thread_Primitives branch (Async.Mutex.swift:149-158) + cosmetic Kernel.Handoff.Cell doc reference (Async.Publication.swift:63) + README §41 spurious dep entry. Bundleable as one commit. Closes brief #4 BLOCKED ON INVESTIGATION → RESOLVED. Q2/Q3/Q4 deliberately not investigated per stop-condition. No consumer migration required. v1.1.0 refinement (2026-05-04): Evidence A precise framing (the module identifier — not the concept — is what doesn't exist), Evidence D added (cross-file Synchronization-import invariant), Option 5 added and rejected (retargeting at swift-threads or ISO_9945_Kernel_Thread; both are layer violations AND API-incompatible). Secondary observation (surviving Darwin os_unfair_lock branch as [PLAT-ARCH-008c] candidate cleanup) tracked as separable follow-up; not bundled into 0.1.0 closure since [PLAT-ARCH-008c]'s strengthened reading was scoped to named types (Kernel.Descriptor, Kernel.Process.ID, Kernel.Directory.Entry), and Async.Mutex is a forward-looking ecosystem cleanup item rather than a 0.1.0 tag blocker. Execution awaits explicit principal authorization.

documents.topic: Tier 2 cross-package analysis (v1.3.0, status RECOMMENDATION restored after v1.2.0 false-negative deferral) of whether swift-equation-primitives, swift-hash-primitives, and swift-comparison-primitives can be deprecated once SE-0499 lands. The three packages fork Swift.Equatable/Hashable/Comparable specifically because the stdlib protocols required Self: Copyable; SE-0499 (Implemented Swift 6.4) relaxes that. Verification spike at `swift-institute/Experiments/se-0499-stdlib-noncopyable-protocol-conformance/` PASSES against Swift 6.4-dev DEVELOPMENT-SNAPSHOT-2026-03-16-a when the toolchain is selected via TOOLCHAINS=org.swift.64202603161a (env var with bundle identifier). The earlier `xcrun --toolchain '<snapshot-name>' swift build` invocation was a build-config defect — it sets the compiler binary but does NOT redirect SwiftPM's stdlib resolution; the false-negative diagnostics led v1.2.0 to incorrectly defer. The nightly's bundled stdlib `Swift.swiftmodule/.../arm64-apple-macos.swiftinterface` declares `public protocol Equatable : ~Copyable, ~Escapable` (verified by direct grep). Implementation truth: PR #85079 is the umbrella WIP, but the actual landings are split PRs by Ben Cohen — #85746 (Equatable, 2025-12-03), #85747 (Comparable), #85748 (Hashable), plus #85854/#85891 for ~Escapable. Checking only the umbrella PR is insufficient; for SE-0499 the merged-PR truth requires checking the split-PR family (verified against local `/Users/coen/Developer/swiftlang/swift` main, fetched 2026-05-03). Recommendation: Option C-refined — wrap existing fork protocols in `#if swift(<6.4)`, add `#else` branch with `extension Equation { public typealias \`Protocol\` = Swift.Equatable }` etc.; wrap each `+Swift.*` bridge body in `#if swift(<6.4)` (under 6.4 they duplicate stdlib's existing conditional conformances). Per-package surface preserved: hash-primitives keeps Hash.Value (Tagged<Hash, Int>); comparison-primitives keeps the three-way Comparison enum + Comparison.Compare + Comparison.Clamp + Property.View integrations — substantive value-type surface independent of the Comparable fork. equation-primitives sub-decision (full retirement vs transitional shim) deferred to a separate cycle; this cycle only adds the guards. Per-package guard application requires explicit per-package authorization per feedback_no_public_or_tag_without_explicit_yes. Supersedes the premise of Equation Primitives Design.md v2.1.0 §11.3 and §17.
documents.statusDetail: v1.3.0. Recommendation: Option C-refined — wrap fork protocols in #if swift(<6.4), add #else typealias to stdlib counterpart, wrap +Swift.* bridges in #if swift(<6.4). Verification spike CONFIRMED on Swift 6.4-dev nightly via TOOLCHAINS=org.swift.64202603161a. Per-package guard application authorized for execution by principal direction (do this proactively, test on swift 6.4) — but each per-package commit + push remains a per-action authorization per feedback_no_public_or_tag_without_explicit_yes; the action proceeds inside the workspace and stops at the push boundary unless re-authorized. v1.2.0 was a false-negative deferral due to xcrun --toolchain not redirecting SwiftPM's stdlib resolution; documented in changelog as lesson. Open sub-questions: (a) equation-primitives full retirement vs transitional shim, deferred; (b) Hash.Value survives as one-purpose package or folds into swift-tagged-primitives; (c) Comparison/Compare/Clamp second-consumer check per [MOD-RENT] / [RES-018]; (d) +Identity.Tagged and +Property.View bridge-file per-file audit.

documents.topic: Placement strategy for the llms.txt / llms-full.txt / LLMS.md AI-discoverability conventions across the Swift Institute ecosystem (60+ packages, 17 orgs, swift-institute.org website). Five options analyzed (website-only, repo-only, path-scoped, layered-selective, layered-eager) against three consumer surfaces (web search, code agents, training crawlers). Verifies primary-source claims about the spec status: llms.txt active proposal at llmstxt.org, llms-full.txt is community convention NOT in spec, LLMS.md is draft v0.1, GitHub Issue #18 (multi-property orgs) has no maintainer resolution, retrieval-time crawler behavior is opaque on the consuming side. Recommends Option D — author swift-institute.org/llms.txt + llms-full.txt now (routine repo work, push gated separately per feedback_blog_publish_two_steps), defer per-repo LLMS.md until LLMS.md hits 1.0 OR 2+ major code-agent vendors document fetching it ([RES-018] two-consumers hurdle applied to convention adoption). Updates release-readiness skill with [RELEASE-AI-DISC] one-line append per launch. Re-evaluation trigger: 2026-11-01 or first confirmed vendor disclosure.
documents.statusDetail: Recommendation: Option D layered-selective. Concrete actions enumerated; none authorized yet pending per-action go-ahead. No per-package file ceremony at launch beyond a one-line append to the website llms.txt. Re-evaluate at 2026-11-01 or on first vendor disclosure of llms.txt fetching / LLMS.md spec 1.0, whichever fires first.

documents.topic: Per-paragraph evaluator-lens audit of the four swift-primitives 0.1.0 cohort READMEs (carrier, tagged, ownership, property). Diagnoses the recurring failure mode as **audience inversion** — author-side context (release-process state, design rationale, future-proposal speculation, internal workaround reasoning, ecosystem-convention framing) leaking into prose meant for consumer evaluation. Carrier post-cleanup is the discipline reference (control). Applies a KEEP / COMPRESS / RELOCATE / DELETE verdict per paragraph with line citations and concrete actions. Six surface patterns enumerated; canonical rewrites named per pattern. documents.topic: Design choice for the public-init shape on Carrier (and Tagged via Carrier conformance) given the empirical evidence from the generic-throws-init experiment (swift-tagged-primitives/Experiments/generic-throws-init/, commits 2e0308c and 3686e7c). Two paths analyzed (path c — marker protocol — was rejected by user direction 2026-05-01): (a) keep the current __unchecked:-only discipline with consumers declaring per-domain inits via constrained extensions; (b) extend Carrier with V5 (default extension) or V6 (requirement + default impl) to provide a generic-throws init across all conformers. Decisive axis is validation-mandatory protection: V5/V6 add non-opt-out-able public surface (Swift offers no opt-out for default extensions or default-impl requirements); types whose semantics require validation-mandatory construction lose protection. Recommends path (a) on protection, reversibility, mid-launch-cohort discipline, and [RES-018] premature-primitive grounds.
documents.statusDetail: Recommendation: keep current __unchecked:-only discipline; no Carrier or Tagged source changes. Friction-reduction argument for V5/V6 is real but addresses only the wrapper-with-optional-validation case; consumers can handle this per-domain via V3-style overloaded extensions (the pattern the README already shows). Future revisit conditions documented (3+ packages re-deriving V3-style inits; a Swift language change lifting the H2 refutation; systematic __unchecked: misuse; validation-mandatory-type cases blocked from Carrier).

documents.topic: When an Institute package's design materially derives from a non-owned external upstream Swift package (not transferable via gh api .../transfer), what is the right git/GitHub-level heritage shape? Compares five options (independent re-implementation, fork + publication squash, fork + orphan-replace, mirror-and-sync, transfer-not-applicable) across heritage signal, license-compliance discipline, divergence ergonomics, and composition with the Institute publication-squash workflow. Recommends Option 2 (Fork + publication-squash on top of fork point) as canonical for this case. Includes attribution + license obligations, divergence policy (no upstream merges), and concrete eight-step application to swift-tagged-primitives → fork of pointfreeco/swift-tagged. Complements coenttb-ecosystem-heritage-transfer-plan.md (owned-source case) and git-history-transfer-patterns.md (which contrasts transfer vs fork-then-merge but did not codify the fork case as first-class).
documents.statusDetail: Recommendation: fork-as-heritage (Option 2) for non-owned external upstream with material lineage + permissive license + community overlap. Concrete application to swift-tagged-primitives → pointfreeco/swift-tagged authored as eight-step workflow with per-action authorization gates; not yet executed. To be codified into a swift-package-heritage skill (peer to swift-package).

documents.topic: Empirical survey of five candidate naming patterns for the Windows-side L2/L3 platform stack, triggered by Path X close (2026-04-30) and the user direction that future strict-platform compliance phases raw forms out at L2 entirely (typed-only L2 surface). Five variants tested as 3-target SPM shapes: V1 status-quo (Windows.Kernel.X at both L2 and L3), V2 sub-namespace (Windows.ABI.Kernel.X at L2), V3 twin roots (Win32.Kernel.X at L2, mirrors POSIX ISO_9945/POSIX shape), V4 org-prefix (Microsoft.Kernel.X at L2), V5 literal-spec (WinSDK.Kernel.X at L2). All 16 targets build green; differential signal is architectural. Recommends V3 (twin roots) on spec/policy-separation, [API-NAME-003] specification-mirroring, and POSIX-shape-symmetry grounds. Surfaces the open question of whether the existing POSIX = ISO_9945 typealias should break for cross-platform symmetry (principal decision; not autonomously decided).
documents.statusDetail: Recommendation: V3 twin roots (Win32.Kernel.X at L2 + Windows.Kernel.X at L3). V1 REFUTED architecturally (no spec/policy boundary; future typed-only L2 collision). V2 sub-optimal (ABI sub-tag is not a Microsoft-published spec authority). V4 REFUTED (org-prefix root contradicts [API-NAME-003] spec-mirroring; ecosystem already roots specs at the spec name, not the publisher). V5 REFUTED (Windows-host-only module-vs-type collision invisible to macOS/Linux CI). Open question on POSIX-side typealias-break surfaced for principal decision.

documents.topic: Swift's @_spi-in-@inlinable rule is a direct-reference rule, not a transitive-call-graph rule. Composing @inlinable through a public non-@inlinable boundary severs the cascade. Earlier (2026-04-20) inferential extension that treated propagation as transitive is corrected by this finding. Implications for cross-package consumer migration patterns, error-conversion layering, and overall API-layer design. documents.topic: When multi-cycle audit-remediation arcs land ecosystem-wide changes with parallel/sibling cycles active, what verification gate strategy minimizes latent cross-cycle ambiguities? documents.topic: When does an L3 domain package need its own cross-platform unifier surface (e.g., swift-sockets, swift-io) vs consuming swift-kernel directly (e.g., swift-file-system, swift-paths)? documents.topic: When do new stdlib protocols (introduced in Swift 6.x) become available in SDK .swiftinterface? Three pilot subjects walked: code-surface (27 IDs; 15 mechanical / 6 hybrid / 6 semantic), primitives (7 IDs; 4 / 1 / 2 — includes the canonical mechanical case [PRIM-FOUND-001]), blog-process (23 IDs; 7 / 8 / 8). Aggregate distribution 26/15/16 (≈46/26/28%) across 57 walked requirements. Resistant set 13/57 (22.8%) in three classes: composite multi-mechanism rules (5; HIGH materiality), workflow-vs-outcome rules (5; MEDIUM), external-knowledge-fetch rules (3; LOW). Recommendation: adopt three-class taxonomy with three skill-format clarifications (composite-rule splitting/annotation, workflow-vs-outcome encoding, external-knowledge cost annotation); rewrite ONE reference skill per [SKILL-LIFE-026] before ecosystem-wide rollout; defer GitHub Actions workflow YAML drafting until reference skill stabilizes. Honors supervisor block: no skill files edited, no workflow YAML drafted, scope held to 3 pilots, no token-consuming sweep initiated.

Pilot's Part 1 reconciliation (three-class reduces/extends four-class; complementary levels; no conflict) carries forward unchanged. Resistant-set: 47/236 (19.9%) — slightly smaller fraction than pilot (22.8%) despite tripled absolute count. Three new resistant patterns surface that pilot's three patterns do not cover: (a) **decision-tree wrappers around mechanical predicates** (existing-infrastructure INFRA-020/-024 et al., 6 cases) — outcome mechanical, but rule's value is at-keyboard routing; (b) **API-gap rules** (github-repository GH-REPO-054, 1 case) — verification blocked by absence of upstream API; (c) **reference-table-as-rule** (package-export PKG-EXPORT-010/-011, 2 cases) — non-normative illustration content wrapped in a requirement ID. All three addressable through additional inline annotations (`**Routing:**`, `**API-Gap:**`, `**Reference:**`); no fourth taxonomy class required. Walks 208 requirement IDs across the 7 skills (193 unique once 15 [IDX-*] rules shared between conversions and index are deduplicated to canonical home); aggregate distribution 107 mechanical / 58 hybrid / 43 semantic = 51.4/27.9/20.7% (49.7/29.0/21.2% on the unique-rule view). Tier-2 mechanical density sits between pilot (45.6%) and tier-1 (57.2%) as the brief predicted; **hybrid share peaks at tier-2 (27.9%)** — code-shape rules invite "AST prefilter + intent-of-use judgment" structure more than infra/process rules do. γ-roadmap saturation: 2/107 mechanical rules (1.9%) carry γ-marks (PATTERN-005, PATTERN-006 — Package.swift settings linting); tier-2 is functionally γ-empty as the brief predicted because v1.2.0's γ family targets workflow infrastructure, not SwiftPM target schemas / namespace structure / ownership shape. Latent γ-2 candidate clusters surfaced for future extension: 7 modularization Package.swift schema rules ([MOD-001/005/006/007/012/015/024]), 17 index type-shape rules, 8 memory-arithmetic operator-overload rules — each a distinct deterministic-scan family against a closed schema. The conversions Reference-shape cluster (14 definitional-anchor cases) was the most material UNanticipated finding. Recommendation: adopt pilot+tier-1's six annotations unchanged; optionally adopt the Reference sub-shape disambiguator; no seventh class needed. Tier-2's contribution to workflow-construction substrate is not immediate (only 2 γ matches) — the rules would feed NEW γ-classes (SwiftPM target-shape audit, Swift-AST type-shape audit) not yet in v1.2.0 §3.4. Tier-2's value is taxonomy validation, not workflow input. Workflow construction continues against tier-1 bucket. documents.topic: Tier 3 (FINAL) ecosystem-wide extension of `skill-verification-taxonomy-pilot.md` v1.0.0 + tier-1 + tier-2 across the 24 voice/process skills (collaborative-discussion, corpus-meta-analysis, document-markup, experiment-process, handoff, implementation, issue-investigation, quick-commit-and-push-all, readme, reflect-session, reflections-processing, research-process, skill-lifecycle, supervise, swift-evolution, swift-forums-review, swift-pull-request, testing, testing-institute, testing-swiftlang, dutch-law, legal-encoding, legal-testing, rule-law-core).
